definition: The 2004-06 Greenspan Conundrum is the most informative historical analogue (7.5/10 similarity) for current broken monetary transmission, with specific sequencing: 30 months accumulation → 12 months contained deterioration → 6 months discontinuous repricing (IG from 50bp to 600bp+). Current broken transmission has accumulated ~36+ months.
evidence: Current broken transmission has been accumulating longer than the historical analogue
Signpost hierarchy (IG OAS >75bp from trough) is consistent with Phase 2 detection
open_questions: n=1 for the exact pattern limits statistical confidence
Current vulnerabilities (basis trade, private credit) differ from then (subprime mortgage)
Will the sequencing pattern replicate or has market structure evolved?
